description:
Goal: make the work layer read as the scheduler spine before semantic jobs grow. `work.rs` should own the shared work vocabulary and drive loop; `jobs::` should own job implementations and their private helpers.

Change:
- Keep `Job`, `FactKey`, `JobEffects`, `WorkGraph`, job metadata, and `World::drive` in the work spine.
- Add `compiler2/jobs/mod.rs` as the dispatch surface used by the drive loop.
- Move current source-surface jobs to `compiler2/jobs/source.rs`: `IndexCode`, `ScopeCode`, `DefineModule`, namespace/import helpers, module discovery, function indexing, and diagnostics local to source indexing/scoping.
- Move current root jobs to `compiler2/jobs/root.rs`: `SeedRoot` and the current `CheckSemanticClosure` placeholder.
- Preserve the rule that jobs return `JobEffects`; only the world drive loop applies effects to the work graph.

Shape rule:
- Split by cohesive helper set, not mechanically one file per enum variant.
- A one-job module is right when the job owns substantial private data/helpers (`body::lower_function`, later artifact jobs).
- Shared surface jobs stay together while they share `define_scope`, imports, exports, and module/function definition helpers.
- If `semantic.rs` grows large, split under `jobs/semantic/` by semantic concern (`activation`, `callsite`, `return_need`, `executable`, `closure`) rather than by rote variant count.

Why:
- The next semantic ticket needs body and semantic job families with their own private helpers. Keeping those in `work.rs` would mix source scoping, lowering, semantic analysis, and drive mechanics in one file.
- One-file-per-job for the current source jobs would force helper leakage or duplicate the source-surface algorithm. Family modules keep privacy and make dataflow easier to audit.
- This is a behavior-preserving cleanup that protects the dataflow boundary before adding new facts.

Checks:
- Compiler2 telemetry-driven tests still pass with the same job span names and outcomes.
- `cargo test --lib compiler2::` passes.
- No lowering, semantic analysis, planner, or artifact behavior is added here.
title: Compiler2 work: split job families behind the drive spine
